Commands  ›   ›  Element Alignment Match Slope Intersection

Element Alignment Match Slope Intersection

writes to model Public API · not yet exposed

Rotates each moving element about the closest-approach point of its extended axis and the reference's extended axis, so the two axes end up intersecting with the reference direction.

Before you run this

Active document. 1 reference + ≥1 mover resolved. Both have directional geometry.

After it runs

Each mover rotated so its axis meets the reference axis at a common apex.

When to use this

  • Converging pipe / duct runs whose axes must meet at a specific apex.
Don't use this when

Simple parallel slope matching (use MatchSlopeFromReferenceCommand).

Decision flow

1) Resolve reference + movers. 2) Extract reference direction + start. 3) Transaction: per mover compute closest-approach point of axes, compute ΔZ-rotation, RotateElement about axis Z through that apex. 4) Commit, log. 5) Failure → WireFault + rollback.

What you'll see in the chat

on start
Running ElementAlignmentMatchSlopeIntersectionCommand...
thinking
Validating preconditions...
Applying deterministic steps...
on success
ElementAlignmentMatchSlopeIntersectionCommand completed successfully.
on failure
